english: Sch.: order, succession (?); tsár-du dṅar (tsar du dngar) Lex., Sch. put in order, placed in array.

(col. often *mul* (mul)) 1. silver. -- 2. money. -- 3. a rupee. -- 4. a tola or Indian half ounce; dṅúl-gyi ťóg-nas dṅul ysúm-ču ťob (dngul gyi thog nas dngul gsum cu thob) he gets 30 rupees out of the ready money; dṅúl-k'a (dngul kha) a silver mine, a vein of silver; dṅul-kúg, dṅul-sgyíg (dngul kug, dngul sgyig) moneybag, purse; dṅul-čú (dngul chu) quicksilver, mercury; dṅul-dúl-ma (dngul dul ma) refined silver Sch.; dṅul-rmíg (dngul rmig), lump, bar, ingot, of silver Sch.; *ṅul-zán(s)* (ngul zang(s)) W., C., silvered or plated copper.
1. shore, bank Lexx. -- 2. edge of a knife Cs.; fig. rta-lčág-gi dṅo (rta lcag gi dngo) whipcord, lash of a whip C. -- 3. handle of a knife (??) Cs.
, dṅom-brjíd (dngom brjid), brightness, splendour; dṅóm-po, dṅóm-čan (dngom po, dngom can) shining, bright Cs.; Lex. dṅom-čé (dngom che) very bright. Cf. rṅám-pa (rngam pa).

1. reality, real, dṅos daṅ sgyú-ma (dngos dang sgyu ma) reality and illusion; rgyál-bu dṅos (rgyal bu dngos) the real prince (opp. to a spurious one); proper, true, genuine; positive (opp. to negative) Gram.; personal, dṅós-la yód-pa (dngos la yod pa) to be personally present; dṅós-su (dngos su), resp. žál-dṅós-su (zhal dngos su) bodily e.g. to appear bodily; a-yíg dṅós-su med kyaṅ (a yig dngos su med kyang), even though the a is not actually written there, Gram.; dṅós-su grúb-pa méd-pa (dngos su grub pa med pa) to have no real existence Thgy. -- 2. Cs.: pers. pron. I, cf. ṅos (ngos); dṅos-dzin-pa (dngos 'dzin pa) to be selfish Thgy.; dṅos-dzin (dngos 'dzin) selfishness, selfinterest, dṅos-dzin ydón-gyis zin-pa (dngos 'dzin gdon gyis zin pa) to be possessed by the demon of selfishness Thgy.; dṅos-dzin-čan (dngos 'dzin can) selfish, self-interested. Thus it was explained by Lamas, though it cannot be denied that sometimes the version: belief in existence, a clinging to reality, a signification equally justifiable by etymology (v. below), would be more adequate to the context. -- 3. Tar. 150, 14: thou, you; except in this passage I did not meet with the word in this sense, yet it may be used so, in the same manner as nyíd (nyid) q.v. Comp. and deriv. dṅos-grúb (dngos grub), Ssk. siddhi, 1. perfection, excellence, any thing of superior value, e.g. honour, riches, talents, and esp. wisdom, higher knowledge, and spiritual power, as far as they are not acquired by ordinary study and exercise, but have sprung from within spontaneously, or in consequence of long continued contemplation. This dṅos-grúb (dngos grub) is, as it were, the Buddhist caricature of the χαρίσματα of the N.T. (v. I. Cor. 12,4). -- 2. name of male persons, col. *ṅo-rúb* (ngo rub) W. -- dṅos-ṅán (dngos ngan) having little flesh, ill-fed, emaciated Mil. -- dṅós-čan (dngos can) material, real Cs. -- dṅós-dad (dngos dad) true faith, opp. to blún-dad (blun dad) a fool's faith, superstition Mil. -- dṅos-sdíg (dngos sdig) prob.: real, or still effective sin, unatoned, unexpiated sin Dzl. ༢, 14; or less emphatically: sinful actions in general ༢, 15. -- dṅós-po (dngos po), Ssk. भव, वस्तु, thing, natural body, ser dṅúl-la sógs-pai dṅós-po (ser dngul la sogs pa'i dngos po) Glr.; matter, subject, dgá-bai dṅós-po (dga' ba'i dngos po) matter of rejoicing Wdn.; goods, utensils, dge-slóṅ-gi (dge slong gi) wearing-apparel of a Gelong; occurrence, event, action, dṅós-po sgrúb-pa (dngos po sgrub pa) to bring a thing about, to set it on foot or a going Dzl.; as a philosophical term: substance, matter, Was. (270. 294); dṅós-por dzin-pa (dngos por 'dzin pa) the belief in the reality of existence Mil. -- dṅós-ma (dngos ma) Cs. natural (opp. to artificial), natural productions. -- dṅós-miṅ (dngos ming) 1. the proper or real name for a thing: so Zam. uses the paraphrase: p'o-mtsán-gyi dṅós-miṅ (pho mtshan gyi dngos ming), in order to avoid the plain expression mje (mje), which is considered obscene. 2. noun substantive, Chr. Prot. a newly coined grammatical term. -- dṅos-méd (dngos med) Lex. = Ssk. abhāva, Cs. immaterial, not existing, Was. (281): not real. -- dṅos-slób (dngos slob) a real, a personal pupil Tar. often. -- dṅos-gži (dngos gzhi) (Lex. = Ssk. mūla) the main part of a thing, the thing itself, e.g. the subject-matter of a treatise, the ceremony itself, opp. to sṅón-gro (sngon 'gro) introduction, sbyór-ba (sbyor ba) preparation, and eventually also rjes (rjes) that which follows.
pf. mṅags (mngags), to commission, charge, delegate, send (a messenger, commissary etc.) Dzl.; also used of Buddha's sending a Bodhisatva on the earth to con-
